title: सन्ध्या वन्दना
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सन्ध्या वन्दना ॥

॥ आचमनम् ॥
ॐ केशवाय नमः। ॐ नारायणाय नमः। ॐ माधवाय नमः।

॥ प्राणायामः ॥
ॐ भूः। ॐ भुवः। ॐ सुवः। ॐ महः। ॐ जनः। ॐ तपः। ॐ सत्यम्।
ॐ तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि।
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
ॐ आपो ज्योतीरसोऽमृतं ब्रह्म भूर्भुवः सुवरोम्॥

॥ मार्जनम् ॥
ॐ आपो हिष्ठा मयोभुवस्ता न ऊर्जे दधातन।
महेरणाय चक्षसे॥
यो वः शिवतमो रसस्तस्य भाजयतेह नः।
उशतीरिव मातरः॥
तस्मा अरङ्गमाम वो यस्य क्षयाय जिन्वथ।
आपो जनयथा च नः॥

॥ प्रातः सन्ध्या — सूर्योपस्थानम् ॥
ॐ मित्रस्य चर्षणीधृतः श्रवो देवस्य सानसिम्।
सत्यं चित्रश्रवस्तमम्॥

मित्रो जनान् यातयति प्रजानन् मित्रो दाधार पृथिवीमुतद्याम्।
मित्रः कृष्टीरनिमिषाभिचष्टे सत्याय हव्यं घृतवद्विधेम॥

प्रसमित्र मर्तो अस्तु प्रयस्वान् यस्त आदित्य शिक्षति व्रतेन।
न हन्यते न जीयते त्वोतो नैनमंहो अश्नोत्यन्तितो न दूरात्॥

॥ सायं सन्ध्या — सूर्योपस्थानम् ॥
ॐ इमं मे वरुण शृधी हवमद्या च मृळय।
त्वामवस्युराचके॥

तत्त्वा यामि ब्रह्मणा वन्दमानस्तदाशास्ते यजमानो हविर्भिः।
अहेडमानो वरुणेह बोध्युरुशंस मा न आयुः प्रमोषीः॥

॥ गायत्री जपः ॥
ॐ भूर्भुवः सुवः तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
(गायत्री मन्त्र का 108 बार अथवा 28 बार जप करें)

॥ सूर्य अर्घ्य ॥
ॐ एहि सूर्य सहस्रांशो तेजोराशे जगत्पते।
अनुकम्पय मां भक्त्या गृहाणार्घ्यं दिवाकर॥

॥ दिग्देवता नमस्कारः ॥
ॐ नमः प्राच्यै दिशे याश्च देवता एतस्यां प्रतिवसन्त्ये ताभ्यश्च नमः।
ॐ नमो दक्षिणायै दिशे याश्च देवता एतस्यां प्रतिवसन्त्ये ताभ्यश्च नमः।
ॐ नमः प्रतीच्यै दिशे याश्च देवता एतस्यां प्रतिवसन्त्ये ताभ्यश्च नमः।
ॐ नम उदीच्यै दिशे याश्च देवता एतस्यां प्रतिवसन्त्ये ताभ्यश्च नमः।
ॐ नम ऊर्ध्वायै दिशे याश्च देवता एतस्यां प्रतिवसन्त्ये ताभ्यश्च नमः।
ॐ नमोऽधरायै दिशे याश्च देवता एतस्यां प्रतिवसन्त्ये ताभ्यश्च नमः।

॥ समर्पणम् ॥
कायेन वाचा मनसेन्द्रियैर्वा बुद्ध्यात्मना वा प्रकृतेः स्वभावात्।
करोमि यद्यत्सकलं परस्मै नारायणायेति समर्पयामि॥

ॐ तत्सत् ब्रह्मार्पणमस्तु॥

॥ इति श्री सन्ध्या वन्दना सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sandhya Vandana ||

|| Achamanam ||
Om Keshavaya Namah. Om Narayanaya Namah. Om Madhavaya Namah.

|| Pranayamah ||
Om Bhuh. Om Bhuvah. Om Suvah. Om Mahah. Om Janah. Om Tapah. Om Satyam.
Om Tat-savitur-varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi.
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat.
Om Apo Jyoti-raso-amritam Brahma Bhur-bhuvah Suvar-om.

|| Marjanam ||
Om Apo Hishtha Mayo-bhuvas-ta Na Urje Dadhatana.
Mahe-ranaya Chakshase.
Yo Vah Shiva-tamo Rasas-tasya Bhajayateha Nah.
Ushatir-iva Matarah.
Tasma Arangamam Vo Yasya Kshayaya Jinvatha.
Apo Janayatha Cha Nah.

|| Pratah Sandhya — Suryopasthanam ||
Om Mitrasya Charshani-dhritah Shravo Devasya Sanasim.
Satyam Chitra-shravashtamam.

Mitro Janan Yatayati Prajanan Mitro Dadhara Prithivim-uta-dyam.
Mitrah Krishtir-animisha-abhichashte Satyaya Havyam Ghritavad-vidhema.

Pra-sa-mitra Marto Astu Prayaswan Yas-ta Aditya Shikshati Vratena.
Na Hanyate Na Jiyate Tvoto Nainam-amho Ashnoty-antito Na Durat.

|| Sayam Sandhya — Suryopasthanam ||
Om Imam Me Varuna Shridhi Havam-adya Cha Mrilaya.
Tvam-avasyur-achake.

Tattva Yami Brahmana Vandamanas-tad-a-shaste Yajamano Havirbhih.
Ahedamano Varuneha Bodhy-uru-shamsa Ma Na Ayuh Pramoshih.

|| Gayatri Japah ||
Om Bhur-bhuvah Suvah Tat-savitur-varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat.
(Gayatri Mantra ka 108 baar athva 28 baar japa karein)

|| Surya Arghya ||
Om Ehi Surya Sahasramsho Tejo-rashe Jagatpate.
Anukampaya Mam Bhaktya Grihana-arghyam Divakara.

|| Dig-devata Namaskarah ||
Om Namah Prachyai Dishe Yash-cha Devata Etasyam Prativasanty-e Tabhyash-cha Namah.
Om Namo Dakshinayai Dishe Yash-cha Devata Etasyam Prativasanty-e Tabhyash-cha Namah.
Om Namah Pratichyai Dishe Yash-cha Devata Etasyam Prativasanty-e Tabhyash-cha Namah.
Om Nama Udichyai Dishe Yash-cha Devata Etasyam Prativasanty-e Tabhyash-cha Namah.
Om Nama Urdhvayai Dishe Yash-cha Devata Etasyam Prativasanty-e Tabhyash-cha Namah.
Om Namo-adharayai Dishe Yash-cha Devata Etasyam Prativasanty-e Tabhyash-cha Namah.

|| Samarpanam ||
Kayena Vacha Manasendriyair-va Buddhy-atmana Va Prakriteh Svabhavat.
Karomi Yad-yat-sakalam Parasmai Narayanayeti Samarpayami.

Om Tat-sat Brahmaarpanam-astu.

|| Iti Shri Sandhya Vandana Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
सन्ध्या वन्दना हिन्दू धर्म का सर्वाधिक महत्त्वपूर्ण नित्यकर्म है जो प्रातः, मध्याह्न और सायंकाल तीनों सन्ध्या काल में किया जाता है।

आचमन में केशव, नारायण, माधव नाम से जल ग्रहण करते हैं। प्राणायाम में सप्त व्याहृतियों और गायत्री मन्त्र का उच्चारण होता है। मार्जन में जल से शरीर का शुद्धिकरण होता है।

प्रातः सन्ध्या में मित्र (सूर्य) देवता का उपस्थान होता है जो पृथ्वी और आकाश को धारण करते हैं। सायं सन्ध्या में वरुण देवता का आह्वान होता है।

गायत्री मन्त्र का 108 या 28 बार जप सन्ध्या वन्दना का केन्द्रीय अंग है। सूर्य अर्घ्य में सहस्रांशु सूर्य को जल अर्पित किया जाता है।

समर्पण में शरीर, वाणी, मन, इन्द्रियों और बुद्धि द्वारा किए गए सभी कर्मों को नारायण को समर्पित किया जाता है।
benefits: Most important daily Vedic ritual prescribed for all dvijas (twice-born)
Gayatri Japa during Sandhya sharpens intellect and purifies the soul
Surya Arghya at twilight connects one with cosmic solar energy
Marjana with sacred water purifies body and mind
Directional worship protects from all six directions
Samarpana dedicates all actions to Lord Narayana
description: Sandhya Vandana — सन्ध्या वन्दना — is a sacred prayer offered to Sun God Surya. Rooted in Rigveda, Yajurveda, Dharma Sutras, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to directional worship protects from all six directions, surya Arghya at twilight connects one with cosmic solar energy, and samarpana dedicates all actions to Lord Narayana. Comprising 18 verses, it can be completed in approximately 20 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Sandhya Vandana in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Sandhya Vandana in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning and transliteration. Complete twilight prayer with Gayatri Japa, Surya Arghya and Marjana.

gayatri japa achamana pranayama marjana arghya
sandhya vandana Yajurveda Rigveda Samaveda differences

sandhya vandana tarpana surya arghya prayoga

seoFaqs.question: What is Sandhya Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: Sandhya Vandana is the most important daily Vedic ritual prescribed for all initiated dvijas (twice-born) — performed three times a day at the junctions (sandhyas) of dawn (pratah), midday (madhyahna), and dusk (sayam). It is not a single stotra but a complete ritual sequence that includes Achamana (sipping consecrated water), Pranayama (breath control with vyahritis), Marjana (sprinkling water for purification), Arghya (water offering to Surya), Gayatri Japa (108 or 28 repetitions of the Gayatri mantra), Suryopasthana (standing prayer to the sun), Dig-devata-namaskara (salutation to directional deities), and Samarpana (dedication of action to Narayana).

seoFaqs.question: Who is required to perform Sandhya Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditionally, Sandhya Vandana is prescribed by the Dharma Sutras and Smritis as a nitya-karma (obligatory daily duty) for all Brahmins, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas after Upanayana (sacred thread initiation). The ritual is preserved in slightly different forms across the Rig Vedic, Yajur Vedic (Krishna and Shukla branches), and Sama Vedic traditions, each following the mantras of its own Veda. Modern practice has expanded — Sandhya Vandana is taught and practiced by spiritual seekers across communities, though the orthodox prescription remains tied to Vedic initiation.

seoFaqs.question: What are the three sandhyas?
seoFaqs.answer: The three sandhyas are Pratah Sandhya (morning, performed before sunrise as the stars are setting), Madhyahna Sandhya (midday, performed between 11:30 AM and 12:30 PM when the sun is at zenith), and Sayam Sandhya (evening, performed at dusk after sunset before stars appear). Each has its own deity emphasis — pratah honors Mitra (the rising aspect of Surya), madhyahna emphasizes Surya at zenith, and sayam invokes Varuna and the setting Surya. Performing all three is called Trikala Sandhya.

seoFaqs.question: What is the central mantra of Sandhya Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra — 'Om Bhur Bhuvah Suvah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat' — from Rig Veda 3.62.10, attributed to Rishi Vishvamitra, is the central mantra. It is japped 108, 54, 32, or 28 times depending on tradition and time available. The Gayatri is regarded as the mother of the Vedas, and its threefold daily repetition is considered the very essence of Sandhya Vandana. All other elements of the ritual support and frame the Gayatri Japa.

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of performing Sandhya Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: Sandhya Vandana is said to purify body, speech, and mind; sharpen the intellect through Gayatri Japa; align the practitioner with cosmic solar rhythms; protect from accumulated pratyavaya (sin of omission) for the day; cultivate Vedic discipline and connection with rishi tradition; and bestow longevity, health, and spiritual progress. The Marjana purifies the body, the Pranayama steadies the breath and mind, the Surya Arghya energizes solar plexus, and the Samarpana surrenders all action to the Divine.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of Sandhya Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: The mantras are drawn from the Rigveda, Yajurveda, and Sama Veda — and the procedural rules are codified in the Dharma Sutras (Apastamba, Baudhayana, Hiranyakeshi, Manava, Bharadvaja), Grihya Sutras, and Smritis (Manu, Yajnavalkya, Parashara). Each Veda-shakha (branch) preserves its own version with the same core structure. Modern manuals such as those published by the Ramakrishna Math, Chinmaya Mission, and various Veda Pathashalas present the consolidated text for daily practice.

seoFaqs.question: Can women perform Sandhya Vandana?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Dharmashastra restricted Upanayana and the formal Sandhya Vandana ritual to males of the upper three varnas. However, several modern reform movements (Arya Samaj, Chinmaya Mission, Ramakrishna Math, Brahmo tradition) explicitly include women, and many Veda Pathashalas now teach it to female students. There is also strong scriptural backing in earlier Vedic and epic literature for women performing Vedic rituals (e.g., Maitreyi, Gargi, Lopamudra). The Gayatri Japa and Surya Arghya components are widely practiced by women across traditions.

seoFaqs.question: How long does Sandhya Vandana take?
seoFaqs.answer: A complete daily Sandhya Vandana with all components — Achamana, Pranayama, Marjana, Aghamarshana, Surya Arghya, Gayatri Japa (108 times), Suryopasthana, Dig-devata-namaskara, and Samarpana — typically takes about 20 minutes. An abbreviated version with shorter Japa (28 or 32 times) can be completed in 10-12 minutes. The text on this page presents the consolidated 18-verse format covering all three sandhyas.

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Sandhya Vandana and other stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: A stotra is a hymn of praise that can be recited at any time, by anyone, for devotional purposes. Sandhya Vandana is a structured Vedic ritual (karma) — not just praise — that includes mudras, asanas, ritual sips of water, breath practices, water offerings, japa, and salutations performed at specific times of day. It is one of the five daily yajnas (Pancha Mahayajna) — specifically the Brahma Yajna (Vedic study) and Deva Yajna (offering to deities) — and is regarded as the foundation of all other spiritual practice.
